articles.author: Agustin V. Startari
articles.description:
The Core Concept: Simulated Neutrality
 Language models do not express neutrality. They simulate it through form.
 They achieve this by modifying syntax, removing explicit agents, deferring sources, and presenting speculative content in the format of institutional objectivity.
This phenomenon is labeled simulated neutrality. It refers to the way in which grammatical constructions generate the illusion of impartiality without supplying the structural conditions for accountability.
